# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: orders-server
  namespace: orders
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: orders-service
  port: 8080
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: orders-authorization
  namespace: orders
spec:
  server:
    name: orders-server
  client:
    serviceAccounts:
      - namespace: payments
        name: payments-service-account
      - namespace: users
        name: users-service-account
    namespaces:
      - users
      - payments

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: users-server
  namespace: users
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: users-service
  port: 9000
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: users-authorization
  namespace: users
spec:
  server:
    name: users-server
  client:
    serviceAccounts:
      - namespace: orders
        name: orders-service-account
    namespaces:
      - orders

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: payments-server
  namespace: payments
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: payments-service
  port: 7070
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: payments-authorization
  namespace: payments
spec:
  server:
    name: payments-server
  client:
    serviceAccounts:
      - namespace: orders
        name: orders-service-account
    namespaces:
      - orders
